clinical_history: Asymptomatic 60 year old man with ECG as part of pre-operative assessment. What is the rhythm?
diagnosis: The rhythm is atrial fibrillation with an average ventricular response at rest of about 84/min (range about 70-120/min).  Relatively low voltage is present diffusely, a non-specific finding that may be seen as a physiologic variant, with obesity, anasarca, pericardial effusion or constriction, chronic obstructive lung disease, amyloid cardiomyopathy, etc. Borderline left axis deviation is also present.
